fire_rating.ul_design: Per original UL design listing for the member being repaired

components.name: Existing SFRM
components.description: Original spray-applied fireproofing that remains intact adjacent to the damaged/removed area

properties.condition: Intact, bonded to substrate, at or above specified thickness
properties.edge_preparation: Cut back to sound material — remove all loose, delaminated, or water-damaged SFRM
properties.edge_profile: Square cut or slightly undercut to allow patch material to key in

components.name: Prepared Substrate
components.material: Exposed steel surface at repair area
components.description: Steel surface exposed by SFRM removal. Must be cleaned and prepared before patch application.

properties.cleaning: Remove all loose material, dust, oil, grease, and corrosion products
properties.surface_prep_standard: SSPC-SP2 (Hand Tool Cleaning) minimum, SP3 (Power Tool Cleaning) if corroded
properties.primer_note: Re-prime if original primer was damaged or if bare steel is exposed
properties.moisture_note: Substrate must be dry — no condensation or standing water

components.name: Patch Material
components.material: SFRM — same product as original or manufacturer-approved compatible product
components.description: Repair material applied to restore fire rating. Must be same product or listed compatible alternative.

properties.product_match: Use same manufacturer and product as original SFRM when possible
properties.alternative: If original product unavailable, use manufacturer-approved compatible product with equal or better properties
properties.application_method: Spray-applied for large areas, hand-trowel (tuckpointing) for small patches
properties.density_match: Patch material density must match original — do not mix standard and medium density

components.name: Feathered Edges
components.description: Perimeter of the patch where new material overlaps existing SFRM. Must be feathered for adhesion and continuous thickness.

properties.profile: New material feathered over existing SFRM edge — no abrupt step
properties.bond_note: Dampen (not soak) existing SFRM edge to improve bond with patch material

components.name: Re-Application Zone
components.description: The entire area where SFRM is reapplied to full specified thickness. Zone extends beyond the damage area to ensure overlap with existing.

properties.zone_extent: Damage area plus min 2 inches beyond on all sides
properties.thickness_requirement: Must match or exceed original specified thickness at every point
properties.thickness_verification: Pin gauge measurement after curing — minimum 5 readings per patch

assembly_constraints.description: Patch material must be compatible with existing SFRM
assembly_constraints.rule: Use same manufacturer and product. If unavailable, obtain manufacturer written approval for alternative.

assembly_constraints.description: Exposed steel must be properly prepared before re-application
assembly_constraints.rule: Clean to SSPC-SP2 minimum. Re-prime if primer was damaged. Substrate must be dry.

assembly_constraints.description: Full specified thickness must be restored at all points within the repair zone
assembly_constraints.rule: No point within the repair zone may be less than the specified minimum thickness per UL listing

assembly_constraints.description: Patch must overlap existing SFRM to prevent delamination at the interface
assembly_constraints.rule: Min 2-inch overlap onto existing sound SFRM on all sides of patch

assembly_constraints.description: Repair must be documented — before/after photos and thickness readings
assembly_constraints.rule: Document: location, cause of damage, product used, thickness readings, date, applicator. Per AWCI 12-B.

assembly_constraints.description: Same environmental requirements as original application
assembly_constraints.rule: Ambient and substrate temp min 40F, substrate dry, no wind (if exterior), adequate ventilation
